reason: Enabling FIPS mode requires adding a kernel boot parameter (fips=1) and changing the system-wide cryptographic policy. This change is not supported on an already installed RHEL 9 system and can render the system unbootable or cause critical services to fail, leading to potential lockout. The policy explicitly classifies FIPS changes as too dangerous to remediate automatically.
estimated_impact: reboot required; authentication/authorization affected; Potential boot failure; System-wide cryptographic policy change; Possible loss of SSH/console access; Service disruption due to incompatible crypto modules
